titles.title: Seishun Buta Yarou wa Ransel Girl no Yume o Minai
Seishun Buta Yarou wa Randoseru Girl no Yume wo Minai
bgs movie 3
aobuta movie 3
青春ブタ野郎はランドセルガールの夢を見ない
Rascal Does Not Dream of a Knapsack Kid
Rascal Does Not Dream of a Knapsack Kid
青春豬頭少年不會夢到紅書包女孩
青春猪头少年不会梦到红书包女孩

relatedanime.anime: Seishun Buta Yarou wa Santa Claus no Yume o Minai
Seishun Buta Yarou wa Odekake Sister no Yume o Minai

anime.description: * Based on the 9th volume of the Seishun Buta Yarou light novel series written by http://anidb.net/cr32834 [Kamoshida Hajime] and illustrated by http://anidb.net/cr28844 [Mizoguchi Keiji]. Finally, the day of http://anidb.net/ch94420 [Mai]`s high school graduation has arrived. While http://anidb.net/ch94422 [Sakuta] eagerly waits for his girlfriend, an elementary schooler who looks exactly like her appears before him. Suspicious, and for all the wrong reasons... Meanwhile, Sakuta and http://anidb.net/ch94421 [Kaede]`s http://anidb.net/ch99775 [father] suddenly calls, saying that their http://anidb.net/ch100146 [mother] wants to see her daughter. She was hospitalized because Kaede`s condition had been too much for her to bear, so what could she possibly want now? Source: Yen Press

externalentity.identifier: 青春ブタ野郎シリーズ_(アニメ)

tag.name: seinen
tag.description: Seinen, literally "young man", is one of the main demographic classifications applied to manga, and, by extension, to anime, especially manga-based ones. As the name implies, seinen works are marketed primarily to an audience of young men; the age range commonly understood to be the target of such works goes roughly from 17 to somewhere in the 40s.

tag.name: target audience
tag.description: Anime, like everything else in the modern world, is targeted towards specific audiences, both implicitly by the creators and overtly by the marketing. While categories are associated with specific sex and age groups, this is not a bar on the anime being enjoyed by people who don`t fit into that band; not only do crossover titles exist, but the categories also have a considerable following outside their main demographic, and a person might as well enjoy anime from all categories. Still, does not invalidate the usefulness of marking a title as one of the categories; the same themes will likely be treated differently in anime geared for different audiences. Only one should not jump the gun and assume that a title will be more or less serious or present content in this or that way due to its target audience, or conversely assume the target audience on the content and its presentation alone. As such, the audience categories help form a broad impression of how a title might work with the announced themes, but offer little to no in-depth information about specific content or its treatment. The audience categories originate from manga magazines, which group titles of a same intended audience into a magazine that is advertised as belonging to that category, and so manga-based anime will have the category from their parent work. As for anime based on other works, the audience is often debatable, but comparing information such as time slot to the manga-based anime generally helps figure out the most likely possibility. If the parent material was classified under multiple audiences over time (for example, it was originally shounen, but then as the plot changed and became more mature, it was reclassified as seinen), and the anime adapts parent material time periods covering multiple audiences, we tag all audiences covered by the anime adaptation.

tag.name: original work
tag.description: What the anime is based on! This is given as the original work credit in the OP. Mostly of academic interest, but a useful bit of info, hinting at the possible depth of story.

tag.name: elements
tag.description: Next to Themes setting the backdrop for the protagonists in anime, there are the more detailed plot Elements that centre on character interactions: "What do characters do to each other or what is done to them?". Is it violent Action, an awe-inspiring Adventure in a foreign place, the gripping life of a Detective, a slapstick Comedy, an Ecchi Harem anime, a SciFi epic, or some Fantasy travelling adventure?

tag.name: novel
tag.description: A novel is a relatively long work of narrative fiction, normally written in prose form, and which is typically published as a book. Manga, comics, or visual novels do not fit this description and are not considered novels; including a small number of illustrations, however, is still permissible, which means that light novels are considered novels.

tag.name: romance
tag.description: Romance describes a story that deals with love between two or more characters typically also having an optimistic ending. Romance is also a difficulty encountered when creating a harem.

character.name: Sakurajima Mai
character.description: Sakurajima Mai is a 3rd year student at Minegahara High School—well known, famous actress, who is currently on hiatus. Because her life had always revolved around her work in entertainment, she remains isolated at school. She`s serious and polite, but has a strong will. She`s overall levelheaded and very kind and helpful, denouncing Sakuta`s bad reputation as false and also having a very soft spot for Kaede. While she can be snarky, she`s pure-hearted, and sometimes even shy around Sakuta`s lewd jokes. Like Sakuta, she`s also very selfless, sacrificing herself to save her boyfriend`s life and become Shoko`s heart donor, though she will be saved by Sakuta. Source: Official site & Wikipedia

character.name: Azusagawa Kaede
character.description: Azusagawa Kaede is http://anidb.net/ch94422 [Sakuta]`s younger sister. As a result of bullying in the past, she`s unable to leave the house and always house-sitting. She loves pandas and her older brother, while consistently striving to become the ideal little sister. Her daily routine includes waking up Sakuta every morning. Source: Official site

character.name: Azusagawa Sakuta
character.description: Azusagawa Sakuta is a strange 2nd year student in high school, who doesn`t have a cellphone in this day and age. He`s currently an outcast at school due to rumors of a violent past... but he doesn`t seem to care. Sakuta is a nice and helpful person, but also very snarky. He`s definitely blunt, honest and straightforward towards Mai, and prone to lewd jokes that even intimidate her, despite her tendency to tease the guy. Sakuta is a typically selfless hero, and his Hero Syndrome is essentially a consequence of the Adolescence Syndrome that involved his sister Kaede first of all, and later all the other main girls of every arc. Source: Official site & Wikipedia

character.name: Futaba Rio
character.description: Futaba Rio is http://anidb.net/ch94422 [Sakuta]`s classmate who is always cool and calm, the sole member of the Science Club and known as an eccentric person at the school. She`s actually one of the Sakuta`s only friends, and is always willing to give various advice on the Puberty Syndrome. Source: Official site

character.name: Koga Tomoe
character.description: Koga Tomoe is a 1st year student at Minegahara High School, a typical high-school girl who can take hints, but is slightly scatterbrained. Being overly concerned about how people look at her, she hides here Hakata accent, which comes out when she panics or is relaxed. Source: Official site

character.name: Makinohara Shouko
character.description: Makinohara Shouko is a shy middle school girl, but reliable and kind. She bumps into http://anidb.net/ch94422 [Sakuta], while holding an umbrella over an abandoned cat in the rain. Source: Official site

character.name: Toyohama Nodoka
character.description: Toyohama Nodoka is http://anidb.net/ch94420 [Mai]`s half-sister, related through her father, a member of the idol group Sweet Palette and designated as the most fashionable within the group. Despite her flashy looks, she attends an elite all girls` high school. She`s confident and can be competitive. Source: Official site

character.name: Ransel Girl
character.description: A mysterious girl carrying a knapsack on her back. Sakuta encounters her at Shichirigahama beach as he waits for Mai`s graduation ceremony to finish. She looks just like Mai did when Mai was a child actress. Source: Official page

character.name: Akagi Ikumi
character.description: A first-year student in the nursing department at the same university as Sakuta. She was Sakuta`s classmate in middle school, and they met again at the university entrance ceremony. She is a diligent and honest honor student, and started a volunteer group at the university to provide academic support to children who are not attending school. She seems to still be worried about Sakuta after he was isolated for speaking out about the existence of Puberty Syndrome in middle school… Source: Official page
